title: WOS API Common Definitions
description: Canonical definitions shared across every public WOS REST API resource schema. Hoists `ActorRef` (ADR 0082 D-9) and `WosResourceUrn` (ADR 0082 D-4) so adding a new entity-type literal or principal class touches one file, not the entire api/ family. Per ADR 0082 D-14, every other api/*.schema.json `$ref`s the absolute `$id` (e.g. `https://schemas.formspec.io/wos-api/_common/v1#/$defs/ActorRef`); inline redefinition is a contract bug. The `_` prefix marks the file as a definitions-only module rather than a resource — it has no resource shape and no top-level oneOf, only the shared `$defs` table.

ActorRef.description: Stable actor reference from the identity/governance subsystem (ADR 0082 D-9). The principal-class segment is the closed VISION §V taxonomy `human | service-account | workload | support`. Identity details live once in the identity/governance subsystem; every record references actors by URN. Replaces the legacy nested `actor: {id,type,name}` shape from prior portal art.

WosResourceUrn.description: Public WOS API resource URN (ADR 0092 D-1). Shape `urn:wos:<typeid>` — 3-segment URN whose namespace-specific string IS the TypeID. Strip `urn:wos:` to extract the canonical TypeID. The TypeID prefix carries the record family (`case | prov | gov | ai | assurance`); sub-resources (tasks, delegations, holds) are identified by URL path context, not independent TypeIDs. Task sub-resources append `/tasks/{taskId}` to the parent instance URN.
